title: `always_ff` LoweringOption isn't correct
body:
Per @youngar 's comment in [this PR](https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/1684):

> We [originally disabled](https://github.com/llvm/circt/pull/1310) always_ff generation because the way we were using it was illegal in some tools: you can't drive a value in both an initial block and an always_ff block. At the time I thought it might be a badly behaving tool, but it turns out this is the correct way to interpret the spec. This is a really unfortunate limitation, we might be able to get around it by changing how initialization works.

> I don't think we have a solid plan of action yet. I'm a little hesitant to turn on always_ff when we know the generated verilog is broken. I could enable the always_ff flag but only generate regular always statements when lowering from FIRRTL.

This isn't correct - the IR should have well defined semantics and be lowerable to different things for different tools - right now it sounds like we're using LoweringOptions because our previous lowering was incorrect.

It sounds like we're generating `sv.alwaysff` in invalid cases, and that lowering should be changed to make `sv.always` instead.  Given that change, we can rip out the LoweringOption.
